calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-26-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Wrobel, for and with petitioner Karl Uebel; Janet Uebel.

The Court noted that there were two issues to be addressed: the validity of the waiver of isnterest by Otto Uebel, and the request for extraordinary fees. Mr. Wrobel reported that since the death of her mother Annamarie Uebel in 1985, Janet Uebel lived alone in the family home and it was always treated as her property, as she paid all the property taxes and all the costs of maintenance. Janet resided alone in the house until 1999, when health problems caused her to move in with her brother Otto, who had lived away from that home since 1955. The property sat vacant from 1999 until March of 2017, when the City sent a Notice of Violation due to the apparently abandoned property which was now unfit to live in. Because of the decrepit condition of the house, it was torn down. A meeting was held in the office of Mr. Wrobel with Karl, Otto and Janet present in late March of 2017, when Otto was advised of his interest in the property and of the need to probate their mother's estate in order to deal with it. Otto said he did not want anything to do with the property, and signed his disclaimer of interest shortly thereafter in May of 2017.
The Court asked Mr. Wrobel if he had anything to add for the Court's consideration regarding his request for extraordinary fees. Mr. Wrobel responded that he did not have time records available, and that he had never previously requested extraordinary fees in a probate proceeding. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Wan. (Not reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-20-2017 Continued To Dec-26-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-06-2017 Continued To Dec-20-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-30-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Wrobel for petitioner Karl Uebel.

The Court raised the issue of notice to the two co-heirs who were original petitioners, but were not included as petitioners in the amended petition. Mt. Wrobel stated that it was his error not to have given proof of notice as to them, but that he still represented them as well as petitioner Karl Uebel. The Court also noted that box 5a on the petition, but that it could be inferred from the checked box 5a(2). He admonished counsel to take greater care incompleting petitions in the future. The Court granted the petition (order signed).

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Jaime. (Not reported)
